title: Blood DNA Extraction Protocol for T. cruzi Detection via LAMP-PCR
author: Sneider Gutierrez

steps.instruction: Equipment · Vortex mixer Centrifuge (capable of 12,000 × g) Heater (prewarmed to 70 °C) Timer
Add 300mL of fresh whole blood in an EDTA to 2mL microcentrifuge tube
Add 1mL of cold isopropanol to the recovered supernatant
Add 1 mL of 70% ethanol to wash the pellet
Store at 4°C for short-term (< 1week) or -20°C for long-term storage (>1 week).
Take out the LAMP-PCR mix stored at -20°C and spin it down. Note: include a negative and positive control.
Cold Storage · A bucket with ice

Reagents & Buffers CTAB 2% lysis buffer (CTAB: 2% (w/v), Tris-HCl (pH 8.0): 100 mM, EDTA: 20 mM, NaCl: 1.4 M. 5 M NaCl (cold/onice) Isopropanol (cold/onice) 70% ethanol PCR-grade nuclease-free water (incubate at 70 °C)

Consumables & Labware · Rack for 2 mL tubes 2 mL microcentrifuge tubes (Catalog 1420-2700 USA Scientific; two per sample) Do not use low-binding tubes. 1 mL pipette + tips 100 μL pipette + tips Paper towel Dampen a paper towel with 10% bleach to cleangloves between samples

Labeling & Organization · Markers for labeling Label the tubes as follows:o Left side: Date and operator’s initialso Top: Sample order (e.g., 1, 2, 3) + “NA” =nucleicacids + full sample nameo Right side: Leave blank for DNA concentration in case you have the equipment to quantify
Add 500 µL of CTAB lysis buffer to the whole blood.
1 min on ice to improve protein precipitation
Incubate at 65°C for 10 minutes
5 seconds Vortex thoroughly to mix.
Add 500 µL of 5 M NaCl to the lysed sample by pipetting three times.
5 seconds Vortex
Centrifuge at 12,000x g for 2 minutes.
Carefully pipette 1 mL of the supernatant from top to bottom, ensuring that the pellet is not touched.
Transfer the 1 mL supernatant to a new, pre-labeled 2 mL tube.

Gently invert to mix 20 times Note: At this point, you should see debris floating in the solution. Trick: If you are working with multiple samples, place the tubes in the rack. Use the palms of your hands to make a sandwich, and gently rotate from right to left to mix the isopropanol

Carefully remove the supernatant by pipetting, making sure not to disturb the pellet stuck to the side at the bottom of the tube. Since the DNA is concentrated in the pellet, you can safely discard any blood debris present in the upper phases.
Centrifuge at 12,000xg for 5 minutes
Centrifuge at 12,000x g for 2 minute s
Vortex for 1 second.

Discard the ethanol by inverting the tube and tapping it repeatedly on a paper towel until any remaining ethanol drains down the side. Note: It is recommended to invert the tube upside down only once. While inverting, gently rotate the tube to prevent the liquid from putting pressure on one side of the tube.
Dry the DNA for 3 minutes at 70 °C.
Resuspend the dried pellet in preheated 25-50 µL by pippeting 20 times.
Incubate at 70 °C for 5 minutes to improve DNA dissolution
Vortex for 5 seconds
Centrifuge at 12000 for 1 minute
Quantify DNA by NanoDrop, taking DNA from the top of the solution.
Add 1 mL of 100% ethanol to wash the pellet
Discard the ethanol by inverting the tube and tapping it repeatedly on a paper towel until any remaining ethanol drains down the side.
Add 2 µL of SYBR Green to the lid.
Spin it down and record the result: brown = negative, green = positive.
Incubate for 1 hour at 65 °C.
Load 5 µL of pre-extracted DNA and pipette 5 times.
Are you running the LAMP-PCR right after? If so,... Make sure you have enough master mix vials pre-stored at -20 °C. Thaw the SYBR Green at 4 °C.
Trick: If you did not see precipitation during the previous step, incubate the sample on ice or -20 °C for 10 minutes before proceeding.
